name: Steal Application Access Token
description: Adversaries may steal application access tokens as a means of acquiring credentials to build persistence or escalate privileges. OAuth tokens, service principal secrets, and SAML assertions can be harvested via AiTM (Adversary-in-the-Middle) proxy frameworks (e.g., Evilginx2, Modlishka), malicious OAuth app consent phishing, or post-compromise credential scraping. In Microsoft 365 / Entra ID, a stolen access token allows replay without MFA because the authentication step already completed — the adversary skips the credential layer entirely and operates as the legitimate user or service principal.
iocs: SigninLogs entries with TokenIssuerType == 'AzureAD' but IPAddress mismatched from prior session
OAuth consent events in AuditLogs with Operation 'Consent to application' for high-privilege scopes (Mail.Read, Directory.ReadWrite.All)
New OAuth app registration followed immediately by credential addition and token issuance
UserAgent string from token replay differs from original session UserAgent
ResultType 0 with AuthenticationRequirement 'singleFactorAuthentication' on accounts enrolled in MFA — indicates token replay bypassing MFA
Multiple resource access tokens issued in rapid succession for same user from different IPAddress values
AuditLogs Operation 'Add OAuth2PermissionGrant' for an app not previously seen in the tenant
OfficeActivity showing delegated mailbox access from an OAuth app immediately after consent event
evasion_variants: Replay token from a residential proxy IP that partially overlaps with the victim's ASN to avoid impossible-travel triggers
Limit resource access to a single service (e.g., SharePoint only) to avoid broad scope anomaly detection
Use a cloned legitimate app display name and publisher to pass visual inspection of consent prompts
Spread token replay sessions across extended time windows (hours) with normal-looking UserAgent strings
Register the malicious OAuth app under an existing compromised tenant service principal to inherit trusted branding
Request only delegated (not application) permissions to stay under automated high-privilege-scope alerts
Avoid Graph API /me/messages in favor of EWS endpoint access to bypass Graph-specific monitoring
detection_kql_hints: Flag SigninLogs where AuthenticationRequirement == 'singleFactorAuthentication' and UserDetails is member of MFA-required group
Detect IPAddress change mid-session: join SigninLogs on CorrelationId and look for multiple distinct IPAddress values
AuditLogs | where OperationName == 'Consent to application' | where TargetResources has_any ('Mail.Read','Directory.ReadWrite.All','Files.ReadWrite.All')
Look for ResultType == 0 with TokenIssuerType == 'AzureAD' and ConditionalAccessStatus == 'notApplied' on MFA-policy-scoped users
Correlate AuditLogs 'Add OAuth2PermissionGrant' timestamp with subsequent SigninLogs ResourceDisplayName activity within 15 minutes
Flag AppDisplayName values not seen in tenant in prior 30 days that have ResultType 0 token issuances
Use bag_unpack(DeviceDetail) to detect mismatched DeviceId between authentication and subsequent resource access events
